scenario: A retail process sends Slack notifications for high-value orders. The team adds a Slack Outbound Connector to a Service Task. They're configuring authentication -- Slack offers several mechanisms.
question: Which authentication methods does the Slack Outbound Connector typically support?

options.text: 0-based application tokens. ) through the app installation flow. Attempting to use username/password in the Slack Outbound Connector's authentication field would fail at runtime since Slack's API no longer accepts this authentication mode.
SLACK_OAUTH_TOKEN}} placeholder). The token's scope (Bot vs User) is governed by the Slack app installation, not by the connector.

Anonymous access -- sending messages to Slack without any authentication credentials by relying on Slack's public message endpoint. Slack's API does not support anonymous access for any message-posting operation. Every API call that writes to Slack (posting to channels, sending direct messages, uploading files) requires a valid OAuth token with appropriate permission scopes. Even read-only public channel operations require authentication on modern Slack API tiers. The Slack Outbound Connector always requires an OAuth token stored as a Camunda cluster secret.
md:37); Incoming Webhook URLs are a different Slack feature (one way posting endpoints) and are NOT a documented outbound connector auth mode. )

a.text: Incorrect. Slack's outbound-connector auth in Camunda 8 uses an OAuth token issued by an installed Slack app. The token (Bot or User scope, depending on the Slack-app config) is referenced from cluster secrets via {{secrets.SLACK_OAUTH_TOKEN}} (per slack.md:37). Both Bot and User OAuth tokens fit the same Authentication property -- only the Slack-side permission scope differs.

b.text: Correct. Slack removed password-based API access for security.

d.text: Incorrect. Slack Incoming Webhook URLs are not exposed by the Camunda outbound Slack connector's Authentication property (the doc lists OAuth token only on slack.md:37). Don't conflate with Camunda's inbound webhook URLs.

explanation: Slack removed password-based API access for security.
